scenario: Helios Healthcare evaluates 800 tasks per day with a DMN decision named triage_band in Camunda 8.8. The team wants the value produced by the decision under resultVariable to reach the process instance automatically so that downstream user tasks and gateways can read the triage fields without anyone writing custom output mappings on the business rule task.
question: What does Camunda 8 do by default with the value placed in resultVariable by a business rule task?

options.text: The value of resultVariable is stored only as a local variable on the business rule task and is discarded as soon as the task completes
The value of resultVariable is merged into the process instance so that downstream activities can read the decision result without further mappings
The value of resultVariable is written into the parent scope only when an explicit output mapping copies it from a local task variable
The value of resultVariable is propagated only after an input mapping declares which fields should be exposed outside of the business rule task

a.text: Incorrect. The documented default behaviour is to merge the decision result into the process instance, not to discard the value when the business rule task completes.

b.text: Correct. By default the variable defined by resultVariable is merged into the process instance, which is exactly the behaviour the team wants for downstream tasks to read the triage fields.

c.text: Incorrect. Output mappings are optional and customise the default merge behaviour. They are not required for the decision result to reach the surrounding process instance.

d.text: Incorrect. Input mappings transform variables passed into the decision engine before evaluation. They do not control how the result is propagated out of the business rule task.

explanation: Camunda 8 merges the variable referenced by resultVariable from a business rule task into the surrounding process instance by default. Output mappings only customise this behaviour rather than enable it.
